DESCRIPTION: This is a sample description in English.
ABOUT:
Full Stack Developer | Angular | Spring Boot | .NET | DevOps Enthusiast

I build scalable, clean, and efficient web applications, bridging the gap between front-end experiences and back-end logic. My stack revolves around Angular, Spring Boot, and .NET, with a growing fire for DevOps practices — CI/CD, automation, and cloud technologies keep me wired in.

But it's not just about code. When I'm not engineering software, you'll probably catch me pushing weights in the gym, chasing the same discipline and progress I bring into development.

For me, it's all about constant growth — in tech, in strength, and in life.

PROJECTS_LIST.TITLE: GymLite
PROJECTS_LIST.SHORT_DESCRIPTION: A microservices e-commerce platform for browsing, buying, and managing gym equipment.
PROJECTS_LIST.DESCRIPTION:
GymLite is a full-stack e-commerce application for gym equipment, with an Angular storefront, an admin dashboard, product browsing, cart and checkout flows, order creation, payment handling, and customer authentication.

The backend is built as Spring Boot microservices with an API Gateway, Eureka service discovery, centralized configuration, JWT security, Kafka notifications, email templates, PostgreSQL and MongoDB persistence, Flyway migrations, Docker Compose infrastructure, and Zipkin tracing.

PROJECTS_LIST.TITLE: FitGear
PROJECTS_LIST.SHORT_DESCRIPTION: An online coaching platform for programs, client progress, and trainer-client follow-up.
PROJECTS_LIST.DESCRIPTION:
FitGear is a full-stack fitness coaching platform that helps trainers manage clients, assign workout programs, review progress, and keep coaching organized in one place.

Clients can follow their plans, log workouts, and track their progress through a clean Angular interface, while the Spring Boot backend supports the coaching workflow with structured data and secure APIs.

PROJECTS_LIST.TITLE: Talksy
PROJECTS_LIST.SHORT_DESCRIPTION: A lightweight real-time messaging app for private and group conversations.
PROJECTS_LIST.DESCRIPTION:
Talksy is a modern messaging application inspired by the simplicity of WhatsApp, focused on fast and friendly communication.

It supports real-time WebSocket chat, one-to-one conversations, group messaging, and secure authentication with access and refresh tokens. The frontend is built with Angular, Bootstrap, and SCSS, while the backend uses Spring Boot, REST APIs, JWT security, and PostgreSQL.

PROJECTS_LIST.TITLE: Showroom
PROJECTS_LIST.SHORT_DESCRIPTION: Manage inventory, clients, user accounts, and sales history — built with Angular & .NET.
PROJECTS_LIST.DESCRIPTION:
As part of my summer internship, I developed a Management Web Application designed to handle stock, client records, user administration, and sales history in a centralized system.

The platform ensures accurate inventory tracking, reliable client management, and role-based access for different types of web users.

Built with Angular on the frontend for an interactive and user-friendly experience and .NET on the backend for secure APIs and solid business logic, the application streamlines daily operations, improves data consistency, and provides clear insights through historical sales tracking and reporting.

PROJECTS_LIST.TITLE: Book Social Network
PROJECTS_LIST.SHORT_DESCRIPTION: A social platform for book lovers to share their books, built with Angular & Spring Boot.
PROJECTS_LIST.DESCRIPTION:
Book Social Network is a full-stack application that enables users to manage their book collections and engage with a community of book enthusiasts.

It offers features such as user registration, secure email validation, book management (including creation, updating, sharing, and archiving), book borrowing with checks for availability, book return functionality, and approval of book returns.

The application ensures security using JWT tokens and adheres to best practices in REST API design. The backend is built with Spring Boot 3 and Spring Security 6, while the frontend is developed using Angular with Bootstrap for styling.

PROJECTS_LIST.TITLE: Investment Calculator
PROJECTS_LIST.SHORT_DESCRIPTION: A web app to calculate investment growth over time, built with Angular.
PROJECTS_LIST.DESCRIPTION:
The Investment Calculator is a web application designed to help users estimate the future value of their investments based on various parameters.

Users can input details such as the initial investment amount, monthly contributions, expected annual return rate, and investment duration.

The application then calculates and displays the projected growth of the investment over time, providing insights into how different factors can impact the final amount.

This tool is particularly useful for individuals planning their financial future and seeking to understand the potential outcomes of their investment strategies.

Built with Angular, the application features a user-friendly interface that allows for easy data entry and visualization of results.

PROJECTS_LIST.TITLE: Get Fit
PROJECTS_LIST.SHORT_DESCRIPTION: A fitness tracking app to log workouts, built with Angular.
PROJECTS_LIST.DESCRIPTION:
Get Fit is a web application designed to help users track and manage their fitness routines effectively.

The app allows users to create and log various types of workouts, including strength training, cardio, and flexibility exercises.

With an intuitive interface built using Angular, Get Fit provides a seamless experience for users to stay motivated and committed to their fitness journey.

PROJECTS_LIST.TITLE: TO-DO
PROJECTS_LIST.SHORT_DESCRIPTION: A simple task management app to organize your to-dos, built with Angular.
PROJECTS_LIST.DESCRIPTION:
TO-DO is a straightforward web application designed to help users manage their daily tasks efficiently.

The app allows users to create, edit, and delete tasks, providing a simple interface to keep track of what needs to be done.

Built with Angular, TO-DO offers a clean and user-friendly experience, making it easy for users to organize their to-dos and stay productive throughout the day.

QUESTIONS.question: What is the capital city of Brazil?
QUESTIONS.explanation: Brasília is the capital of Brazil, designed by Oscar Niemeyer and established in 1960 to move the capital inland from Rio de Janeiro.

QUESTIONS.question: Which planet is known as the Red Planet due to its reddish appearance?
QUESTIONS.explanation: Mars is called the Red Planet because of its reddish surface, caused by iron oxide (rust) on its terrain.

QUESTIONS.question: Who painted the Mona Lisa?
QUESTIONS.explanation: Leonardo da Vinci painted the Mona Lisa around 1503–1506, famous for its detailed background and enigmatic smile.

QUESTIONS.question: What is the chemical symbol for gold?
QUESTIONS.explanation: Au is the chemical symbol for gold, derived from the Latin word 'aurum,' meaning shining dawn.

QUESTIONS.question: Which country hosted the 2016 Summer Olympics?
QUESTIONS.explanation: Brazil hosted the 2016 Summer Olympics in Rio de Janeiro, marking the first time South America hosted the event.

QUESTIONS.question: What is the largest mammal in the world?
QUESTIONS.explanation: The blue whale (Balaenoptera musculus) is the largest mammal, growing up to 100 feet (30 meters) in length and weighing as much as 200 tons.

QUESTIONS.question: In which year did the Titanic sink?
QUESTIONS.explanation: The Titanic sank on April 15, 1912, after hitting an iceberg on its maiden voyage, resulting in over 1,500 deaths.

QUESTIONS.question: Which programming language was created first?
QUESTIONS.explanation: Fortran, developed in the 1950s by IBM, is one of the earliest programming languages, predating C++ (1980s), Java (1995), and Python (1990).

QUESTIONS.question: What is the longest river in the world?
QUESTIONS.explanation: The Nile River, at approximately 6,650 km, is considered the longest river, flowing through northeastern Africa.

QUESTIONS.question: Who wrote the play 'Romeo and Juliet'?
QUESTIONS.explanation: William Shakespeare wrote 'Romeo and Juliet' around 1597, a tragic play about two young lovers from feuding families.
